what_changed.label: Global inflation breadth remains sticky
what_changed.summary: Inflation breadth remains above recent trend, which keeps real-rate sensitivity and duration pressure in view.
what_changed.implication: This matters economically because persistent inflation can keep policy rates restrictive and reduce the margin for a clean duration rally.

what_changed.label: Credit spreads widened modestly
what_changed.summary: Credit spreads widened modestly over the last week, which is a mild risk-off signal rather than a break in market functioning.
what_changed.implication: This matters economically because wider spreads can tighten financing conditions and weaken the case for taking incremental cyclical risk.

what_changed.label: US dollar strength returned
what_changed.summary: The US dollar strengthened again, which can tighten global liquidity conditions and weigh on externally funded risk assets.
what_changed.implication: This matters economically because dollar strength often travels with tighter financial conditions and a firmer hurdle for broad risk appetite.

why_it_matters_economically: The current mix points to tighter financial conditions rather than a clean pro-growth impulse, so portfolio language should emphasize transmission, funding conditions, and downside ballast instead of a single-factor market story.
why_it_matters_here: Across the current sleeve set, the read is to keep growth-sensitive exposure in review while preserving room for ballast and liquidity sleeves to do their job if the macro pulse stays firm.
review_posture: review: Doctrine restraints are present, so manual review remains required.
what_confirms_or_breaks: More direct evidence on inflation cooling would soften the current caution. Further spread widening or another leg higher in the dollar would confirm the tighter-conditions read.
